REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ANN HUM'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-6 TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING AN AGENCY RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE DEFENDANTS. IT IS INSUFFICIENT TO PLEAD AN AGENCY RELATIONSHIP ON INFORMATION AND BELIEF WITHOUT ALLEGING FACTS THAT SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S BELIEF. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 7 IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT ALL DEFENDANTS CONSPIRED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-18-25 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS EDMUND HUM AND BELINDA HUM'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED. THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT TIME BARRED BECAUSE IT IS NOT CLEAR FROM THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT THAT EACH CAUSE OF ACTION'S VIOLATIVE ACTS OCCURRED BEFORE THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS EXPIRED, AND A CONSPIRACY IS ALLEGED IN CAUSE OF ACTION 7. WITH REGARD TO THE NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION, NONE OF DEFENDANT'S AUTHORITY SUGGESTS THAT AN OVERLAP BETWEEN NEGLIGENCE AND NUISANCE CASES OF ACTION SHOULD RESULT IN SUSTAINING A DEMURRER. TWO OF DEFENDANT'S CASES ARE APPEALS FROM JUDGMENT AND THE THIRD CASE REJECTED A NUISANCE CLAIM ON THE MERITS AFTER APPLYING THE RULES OF NEGLIGENCE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-18-25 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED, IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES PRAYERS FOR RELIEF IN CAUSES OF ACTION 3, 5, AND 7 WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS THAT DEFENDANTS ACTED INTENTIONALLY, WILLFULLY, OR WITH CONSCIOUS REGARD WITH REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 12 OF THE COMPLAINT BECAUSE THE COURT HAS ALREADY RULED THAT IT IS INSUFFICIENTLY PLEADED AND GIVEN PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING DEFENDANTS' AGENCY RELATIONSHIP. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EDMUND HUM AND BELINDA HUM'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

Appearances: Cache Cummings of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara, LLP (949.221.1025) for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OVERRULED. The causes of action are not time barred because it is not clear from the face of the Complaint that each cause of action's violative acts occurred before the statute of limitations expired, and a conspiracy is alleged in cause of action 7. With regard to the nuisance cause of action, none of Defendant's authority suggests that an overlap between negligence and nuisance cases of action should result in sustaining a demurrer. Two of Defendant's cases are appeals from judgment and the third case rejected a nuisance claim on the merits after applying the rules of negligence. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ANN HUM'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

Appearances: Cache Cummings of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara, LLP (949.221.1025) for defendant and moving party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to causes of action 1-6 to allege facts supporting an agency relationship between the Defendants. It is insufficient to plead an agency relationship on information and belief without alleging facts that support Plaintiff's belief. The demurrer to cause of action 7 is OVERRULED because the Complaint alleges that all Defendants conspired. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ANN HUM, EDMUND HUM AND BELINDA HUM'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE
COMPLAINT

Appearances: Cache Cummings of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara, LLP (949.221.1025) for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part. The Court GRANTS Defendants' request to strike the punitive damages prayers for relief in causes of action 3, 5, and 7 with leave to amend because the Complaint does not allege facts that Defendants acted intentionally, willfully, or with conscious regard with requisite specificity. The Court DENIES Defendants' request to strike paragraph 12 of the Complaint because the Court has already ruled that it is insufficiently pleaded and given Plaintiff leave to amend to allege facts supporting Defendants' agency relationship. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Rescheduling On Motion To Strike
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT ANN HUM, EDMUND HUM,and BELINDA HUM Notice Of Rescheduling On Motion To Strike.

Off calendar. This is not a properly noticed motion. On April 21, 2025, the court ordered these motions off calendar for failure to meet and confer, untimeliness under CCP Section 1005, and failure to meet and confer.
